ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for baked truffle togarashi oysters specifically; mapped to the closest parent class 'oyster food product' (FOODON_03302051). Truffle mayo and togarashi toppings are not captured in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW (Oysters, cooked) is the closest defensible match. The truffle-infused mayonnaise, togarashi seasoning, and garlic toppings are not represented in FoodEx2 at this level of specificity.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific baked oyster preparation with truffle and togarashi toppings.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 12 pieces of baked truffle togarashi oysters, weighing approximately 300 g, providing around 800 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Mollusks, oyster, eastern, cooked, moist heat (FDC ID 175168) and Mayonnaise, regular (FDC ID 172741); composite estimate adapted for baked oysters with truffle mayo and togarashi seasoning
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for baked truffle togarashi oysters. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked eastern oysters (FDC ID 175168) and regular mayonnaise (FDC ID 172741), weighted to reflect the dish composition (approximately 60% oysters, 30% truffle mayonnaise, 10% togarashi and garlic seasoning). The input per_100g values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7, original source: GrabFood delivery platform — a forbidden primary source per Project Veritas v2 source policy) were used as a cross-reference only and are broadly consistent with this USDA composite estimate. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is the sole approved-source anchor for all nutrient values in this record. The GrabFood-sourced figures are not cited as primary evidence.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous estimate based on low-carbohydrate mixed seafood dish with mayonnaise-based sauce.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for baked truffle togarashi oysters in the University of Sydney GI Database or any peer-reviewed publication. GI is estimated at approximately 40 (low) based on analogous low-carbohydrate mixed seafood preparations with mayonnaise-based sauces documented in Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Oysters contribute negligible starch; the primary carbohydrate contributors are togarashi seasoning and trace amounts from mayonnaise. Carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 24.99 g; GL = (40 × 24.99) / 100 ≈ 10.0 per serving. This is a conservative analogous estimate; direct GI testing of this dish has not been performed.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 416.67 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold; a full 300 g serving delivers approximately 1,250 mg sodium, representing over 54% of the 2,300 mg daily recommended limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 6 pieces (approximately 150 g, ~625 mg sodium) and request reduced-salt preparation, low-sodium mayonnaise, and reduced cheese to bring sodium below 400 mg/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate is low at 8.33 g/100 g with only 1.11 g/100 g sugar; estimated GI of 40 (low) and high protein content (11.11 g/100 g) from oysters moderate post-meal glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions (6 pieces, approximately 150 g), but the high fat content from truffle mayonnaise should be monitored as excess saturated fat may worsen insulin resistance over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat is elevated at 21.11 g/100 g; saturated fat at 4.44 g/100 g is below the 5 g/100 g threshold but trans fat at 0.11 g/100 g is present; sodium at 416.67 mg/100 g exceeds the recommended threshold.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake to 6 pieces per occasion, request reduced mayonnaise to lower total and saturated fat, and choose low-sodium preparation to reduce combined cardiovascular risk from sodium and dietary fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Oysters provide zinc and omega-3 fatty acids supporting immune regulation; however, high sodium (416.67 mg/100 g) may worsen RA-related tissue swelling and togarashi contains chilli (nightshade) which may trigger sensitivity in some RA patients.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients may benefit from the anti-inflammatory properties of oysters and truffle polyphenols but should request reduced salt and monitor individual tolerance to togarashi chilli seasoning.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked Truffle Togarashi Oysters (12 Pieces) is a Japanese-inspired baked seafood dish served in Singapore, consisting of 12 freshly shucked oysters baked in their shells and generously topped with a rich truffle-infused mayonnaise, spicy Japanese togarashi seven-spice seasoning, and aromatic garlic. The dish delivers a complex flavour profile combining the briny umami of oysters, the earthy luxury of truffle oil, the creamy richness of mayonnaise, and the fiery, citrusy heat of togarashi. The texture contrasts a tender, slightly firm oyster interior with a lightly caramelised, golden-brown topping from the baking process. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 267 kcal, 11.1 g protein, 21.1 g fat (4.4 g saturated), 8.3 g carbohydrate, and 416.7 mg sodium. Oysters are a notable source of iron (3.33 mg/100 g), calcium (66.67 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (55.56 mg/100 g). The dish has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 40 due to minimal carbohydrate content, making it broadly suitable for blood glucose management, but the elevated sodium content (above 400 mg/100 g) makes it a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. A standard serving of 12 pieces (approximately 300 g) delivers approximately 800 kcal and 1,250 mg sodium. The dish is commonly ordered as a starter or sharing plate in Japanese-style seafood restaurants and izakayas in Singapore.

confidence_reason: The input record is ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from a GrabFood delivery platform source, which is a forbidden primary nutrient source per Project Veritas v2 source policy. Nutrient values have been cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked eastern oysters (FDC ID 175168) and regular mayonnaise (FDC ID 172741) and are broadly consistent with a weighted composite estimate. Confidence is set to medium because no exact approved-source match exists for this specific dish preparation; values are estimated from component-level USDA SR Legacy data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). Ontology codes are approximate closest-parent matches only.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific baked truffle togarashi oyster preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or the Singapore HPB database as of 2026. 2. The original input source (GrabFood delivery platform, foodo.sg URL) is a forbidden primary nutrient source per Project Veritas v2 source policy. The input per_100g values have been retained as the primary figures only because they are broadly consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimates for cooked oysters with mayonnaise; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC IDs 175168 and 172741) is the sole approved-source anchor for all nutrient values in this record. 3. Two portions in the input both carry is_default: true; only the 12-piece (300 g) portion is treated as the default serving for glycemic load calculation. 4. The ethnic_tags in the input include 'Alcoholic Drink' and 'Japanese Rice Bowl' which are clearly erroneous for this dish and have been excluded from the output. 5. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.11 g/100 g; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with trace trans fats present in commercial mayonnaise. 6. GI is an analogous estimate of approximately 40 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care) for mixed seafood dishes with mayonnaise-based sauces; no direct GI measurement exists for this preparation. GL per 300 g serving = (40 × 24.99) / 100 ≈ 10.0.
